test_not_tautological.reason: A stub implementation that simply writes the expected text patterns into the files without any actual logic would pass all fail-to-pass tests. For example, writing 'public macro fun map_mut' with 'o.borrow_mut()' in the source file and test functions containing '*x = 100', 'option::some(5u64)', etc. would satisfy all text-matching assertions. anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/sui (accessible in container), and the agent could read solve.sh or the solution patch. Additionally, since all fail-to-pass tests are text-matching only, the agent could grep test_outputs.py for the exact strings being asserted and simply insert them into files without understanding the code.
